title: Secret Storage Interface: Local AES-256-GCM Backend and HashiCorp Vault Backend
description: Implement a SecretStorage abstraction with two concrete backends: (1) a local encrypted file backend using AES-256-GCM with scrypt key derivation for development/testing, and (2) a HashiCorp Vault KV v2 backend for production. All private key material for DIDs and all signing keys must go through this interface exclusively. No direct environment variable access for secrets.
objectives: Single SecretStorage protocol that both backends implement identically
Local backend uses scrypt-derived key from environment master key — no plaintext secrets on disk
Vault backend uses AppRole authentication with short-lived tokens
Selection between backends via SENTINEL_SECRET_BACKEND=local|vault environment variable

implementation_guidance.overview: Define SecretStorage as a Python Protocol (structural typing) / ABC in libs/common/secret_storage/. Implement LocalSecretStorage (AES-256-GCM, scrypt) and VaultSecretStorage (hvac library, AppRole auth). A factory function select_backend() reads SENTINEL_SECRET_BACKEND and returns the appropriate instance. Async interface using asyncio for I/O-bound Vault calls.
steps: Step 1 - SecretStorage Protocol: Define in libs/common/secret_storage/base.py. Methods: async get(key: str) -> bytes, async set(key: str, value: bytes) -> None, async delete(key: str) -> None, async exists(key: str) -> bool. Key naming convention: 'sentinels/{service_id}/{role}/{env}/did_private_key', 'sentinels/{service_id}/{role}/{env}/signing_key'.

Step 2 - LocalSecretStorage — Key Derivation: Master key provided via env var SECRET_STORAGE_MASTER_KEY (hex-encoded 32 bytes). Use scrypt for key derivation: n=2^17, r=8, p=1, dklen=32. Salt is stored per-secret alongside the ciphertext. Never store the master key in any persistent file.

Step 3 - LocalSecretStorage — Storage Format: Each secret stored as a JSON file at SECRET_STORAGE_PATH/{key_hash}.enc. File structure: {"version": 1, "alg": "AES-256-GCM", "kdf": "scrypt", "kdf_params": {"n": 131072, "r": 8, "p": 1}, "salt": "<hex>", "iv": "<hex>", "ciphertext": "<hex>", "tag": "<hex>"}. key_hash = SHA-256(key)[:16] used as filename — do NOT store the raw key name in the filename.

Step 4 - LocalSecretStorage — Encryption: Use Python cryptography library. AES-256-GCM via AESGCM(derived_key). iv = os.urandom(12). Authenticate the full JSON metadata as AAD (all fields except ciphertext and tag).

Step 5 - LocalSecretStorage — File Locking: Use asyncio.Lock per key path to prevent concurrent writes. On write, use write-then-rename pattern (write to .tmp, rename atomically) to prevent partial writes.

Step 6 - VaultSecretStorage — AppRole Authentication: vault_addr, vault_role_id, vault_secret_id from environment. On startup, call POST /v1/auth/approle/login. Cache the client_token and renewable=true lease. Implement token renewal loop (renew at lease_duration * 0.75). Re-authenticate if renewal fails.

Step 7 - VaultSecretStorage — KV v2 Operations: Mount path: secret/ (configurable). For get: GET /v1/secret/data/{key} → data.data.value (base64 decode). For set: POST /v1/secret/data/{key} with {"data": {"value": base64(bytes)}}. For delete: POST /v1/secret/metadata/{key}.

Step 8 - VaultSecretStorage — Error Handling: HTTP 404 → raise SecretNotFoundError(key). HTTP 403 → raise SecretAccessDeniedError(key). Connection error → raise SecretStorageUnavailableError. Never expose Vault internal errors to callers — wrap and log.

Step 9 - Factory and Configuration: select_backend(settings: SecretStorageSettings) -> SecretStorage. SecretStorageSettings: backend: Literal['local', 'vault'] = 'local' (from env SENTINEL_SECRET_BACKEND). Validate at startup — fail fast with clear error message if required config is missing.

Step 10 - Unit Tests: Test LocalSecretStorage: round-trip (set then get), tampered ciphertext raises ValueError, missing key raises SecretNotFoundError, concurrent writes are safe. Test VaultSecretStorage: use respx or pytest-httpx to mock Vault HTTP API. Minimum 80% coverage.

methods.name: get
methods.signature: async def get(self, key: str) -> bytes

methods.name: set
methods.signature: async def set(self, key: str, value: bytes) -> None

methods.name: delete
methods.signature: async def delete(self, key: str) -> None

methods.name: exists
methods.signature: async def exists(self, key: str) -> bool

exceptions.name: SecretNotFoundError
exceptions.description: Key does not exist in storage

exceptions.name: SecretAccessDeniedError
exceptions.description: Permission denied — Vault 403 or file permission error

exceptions.name: SecretStorageCorruptedError
exceptions.description: Decryption or integrity check failed — tampered ciphertext

security_constraints: Master key MUST be exactly 32 bytes (64 hex chars) — validate length at startup, fail with clear error
scrypt parameters MUST NOT be weakened below n=2^17 in production — test-only override allowed with TEST_WEAK_KDF=1 flag
Local storage directory MUST have permissions 0700 (owner only) — set programmatically on first use
Secret keys (bytes) MUST be zeroed from memory after use — use cryptography.hazmat.primitives.keywrap or explicit memset equivalent
Vault token MUST NOT be logged — use log masking (***) for all token values in structured logs
AES-GCM IV/nonce MUST be cryptographically random — never reuse IV with the same key (scrypt salt ensures key uniqueness per secret)
GCM authentication tag verification failure MUST raise immediately and MUST be logged as a SECURITY_ALERT event

acceptance_criteria: LocalSecretStorage: set(key, value) then get(key) returns identical bytes
LocalSecretStorage: tampered ciphertext raises SecretStorageCorruptedError on get
LocalSecretStorage: all .enc files have directory permissions 0700
VaultSecretStorage: all operations succeed against a mocked Vault HTTP API
select_backend with SENTINEL_SECRET_BACKEND=local returns LocalSecretStorage instance
select_backend with SENTINEL_SECRET_BACKEND=vault returns VaultSecretStorage instance
Missing SECRET_STORAGE_MASTER_KEY causes startup failure with a clear error message
